name: Jeremy Ruckert
headline: Efficient blocker, but the targets just aren't there
body:
Ruckert's 2025 line tells the whole story: 23 catches on 29 targets across 14 games for 179 yards and a single score, good for just 23.9 fantasy points and a TE54 finish. The peripherals are quietly competent, a 79.3% catch rate, 0.157 EPA per play and a 44.8% success rate, but the volume cap is brutal. A 6.1% target share, 38.8% route rate and 0.84 yards per route run mean he simply isn't being asked to do much in the passing game, and three red-zone looks won't change that.

The profile here is a blocking tight end who occasionally chips in, not a fantasy contributor. Outside projections peg him near 20 catches, 146 yards and barely 22 points, reinforcing a TE85-ish draft tag. Until New York carves out real route volume for him, he stays off redraft boards in every format and only matters in the deepest two-tight-end leagues.
